title: Flowise resetPassword authentication bypass via null/empty reset token
description: AccountService.resetPassword() in packages/server/src/enterprise/services/account.service.ts looks up the target account by email and then checks the password-reset token with `if (user.tempToken !== data.user.tempToken)`. It never verifies that a reset token was actually generated for the account: for a user who has never requested a reset (or who reset before), the stored tempToken is null or an empty string. An attacker who knows the victim's email can call POST /api/v1/account/reset-password supplying a matching null/empty tempToken, passing the equality check. The only remaining gate is an absolute-value expiry comparison against tokenExpiry; for freshly created accounts tokenExpiry defaults near account-creation time, so `Math.abs(now.diff(tokenExpiry,'minutes')) > 15` is satisfiable within the default 15-minute window, letting the attacker set an arbitrary new password and take over the account.
